title: Heritage Events Page
assignees: Chris Traynor, Justyna Acosta, Keith Bruss
description:
Shawn Burgo from APEX sent us an email about the Heritage Events page. He believes there were changes to the page in April that aren't reflected on the new site. I don't see any tasks mentioning updates to this page, but RicketyRoo still had access to update the site before the new site was launched. Wayback Machine did not run a crawl during the time period in question.

Client is requesting that either the content be restored, or a screenshot of the events page before the site was launched.

I don't like the tone of this email. It's debatable whether this is in scope or not. Are we responsible for tracking changes RicketyToo may have made to the old site just before launch of the new site? If so there are probably several things that were changed, it seems to me they were making active updates up to launch. I am thinking we need to request a list of all changes RicketyRoo made to the site prior to launch. It's not possible for us to track have tracked these. The most we could have done is pulled the site info just before migration. I don't think that is feasible with our tight design/dev timelines though.

do you have files from just before the new site was launched that could be reviewed? Would it take significant time to run those to check this?
